title: Cryptographic Hashes, SHA-2, SHA-3, and Commitment Boundaries · Execution Trace: Crypto Go Streaming Hash
summary: A download service calls SHA-256 encryption, truncates digests without analysis, uses MD5 for adversarial integrity, and hashes ambiguous JSON. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the streaming hash increment in hash-purpose and digest verification contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Hash bounded streams incrementally, propagate read errors, freeze canonical byte identity, and compare expected digests safely.
Select supported SHA-2 or SHA-3 functions and output lengths from protocol, interoperability, performance, compliance, and security needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Streaming Hash · Theory Crypto Go Streaming Hash
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 5979 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a hash by purpose while rejecting weak algorithms, ambiguous bytes, and unauthenticated digest claims; preserve accessible status, non-color meaning, keyboard operation, error recovery, and secret-safe diagnostics; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Hash bounded streams incrementally, propagate read errors, freeze canonical byte identity, and compare expected digests safely.
